info.name: Pocket Inspector API
info.description:
# Pocket Inspector API

This collection supports the new building assignment workflow.

## Core Rule
- ADMIN creates sites and buildings
- ADMIN assigns buildings to photographers
- Photographer accounts use API role `INSPECTOR` and must accept before workflow access unlocks
- photographer final fieldwork completion is done via `buildings/:buildingId/surveys/:surveyId/complete-fieldwork`
- photographers can preview readiness with `buildings/:buildingId/surveys/:surveyId/fieldwork-readiness` and optionally bulk-submit valid draft doors via `{ "autoSubmitValidDoors": true }`
- photographers can also partially progress with `buildings/:buildingId/surveys/:surveyId/submit-doors` for selected ready doors without completing the survey

## Consumer Sections
- `🖥️ Admin Portal Endpoints` groups the intended admin portal requests
- `📱 Photographer Mobile Endpoints` groups the intended mobile photographer requests
- Resource folders below remain the canonical module-by-module reference and QA surface

## Recommended Run Order
1. Login as Admin
2. Create site/building(s)
3. Use Building Assignment Workflow -> Admin Portal Flow
4. Login as Photographer
5. Use Building Assignment Workflow -> Photographer Mobile Flow to accept
6. Use Floors and Doors after acceptance
7. Photographer can edit door photos only while the door is in DRAFT
8. After submit, ADMIN can certify or reopen the door for changes
9. If a certified door needs photo changes, ADMIN deletes the certificate, then reopens the door
10. Use survey endpoints separately

## Roles
- ADMIN — manages sites, buildings, assignments, certificates, exports
- Photographer accounts use API role `INSPECTOR` and only work on accepted assigned buildings

## Preferred Flow
- Prefer `building-assignments` + `surveys` for the current admin/photographer workflow
- `building-assignments/*` + `buildings/:buildingId/surveys/*` are the only supported workflow endpoints

## Response Format
All responses: `{ "data": { ... } }`
History responses: `{ "data": [...], "meta": { ... } }`
All errors: `{ "statusCode", "message", "error", "path", "timestamp" }`
## Door Review Rule
- Door photo upload/register/delete is allowed only while the door is `DRAFT`
- `POST /doors/:id/submit` locks photo edits by moving the door to `SUBMITTED`
- ADMIN can return `SUBMITTED -> DRAFT` using `POST /doors/:id/reopen`
- If survey fieldwork was already completed, `POST /doors/:id/reopen` also reopens the active survey back to `IN_PROGRESS`
- If a building certificate already exists for the active survey, ADMIN must delete that before reopening fieldwork or downgrading any certified door
- If a certified door needs photo changes, ADMIN must first delete the certificate (`CERTIFIED -> SUBMITTED`) and then reopen the door (`SUBMITTED -> DRAFT`)

item.name: 🖥️ Admin Portal Endpoints
item.description: Curated admin portal requests. Use this section for setup, team management, buildings, assignment workflow, survey lifecycle, certification, and exports.

item.name: 🔑 Auth & Org
item.description: Admin login, session management, and organisation settings.

exec: // Save deviceId returned by server (auto-detected from User-Agent)

request.description: Login with admin credentials. Only email and password are needed — device info (deviceId, deviceName, deviceType) is auto-detected from the User-Agent header. The server returns deviceId in the response and it is saved automatically.
Exchange a refresh token for a new access + refresh token pair. Token is rotated on each call — old refresh token is invalidated immediately.
Returns the profile of whoever is currently logged in (based on the Bearer token).
Shows all devices currently logged in as the current user — deviceName, deviceType, IP address, last used time.
Kicks a specific device. That device gets 401 on its next API request and must log in again.
Logs out from all devices. Use ?keepCurrent=true to stay logged in on the current device.
Revoke the current session. You must log in again after this.

exec: // Re-login as Admin after logout so subsequent requests still work
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ email: adminEmail, password: adminPassword }) }

request.description: Returns the organisation the current user belongs to.
Update the organisation name. Admin only.
Returns all users in the org. Admin only.

exec: // Generate unique email to avoid 409 conflicts

request.description: Create a new user. Set role to ADMIN or API role `INSPECTOR` for photographers. A random email is generated to avoid conflicts on repeated test runs.
Get a specific user by their ID.
Update a user's name or role.
List all clients in the org. Admin only. Returns clients sorted by name.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Acme Fire Safety Ltd",
  "contactName": "John Smith",
  "contactEmail": "john@acme.com",
  "contactPhone": "+44 20 7946 0958",
  "address": "123 King Street, London EC2V 8AA",
  "notes": "Key account — monthly billing"
}

request.description: Create a new client. Only `name` is required — all other fields are optional. Name must be unique within the org. Saves clientId.
Get a specific client with its linked sites and standalone buildings.
Update client details. Name must remain unique if changed.
Delete a client. This is permanent. Linked sites and buildings will have their clientId set to null.

item.name: 🏗️ Sites & Buildings
item.description: Site and building setup, edits, and current-building certificate operations.

request.description: List sites. ADMIN sees all sites in the organisation. Photographer accounts only see sites that currently contain accepted building assignments.
Create a new site. ADMIN only. Saves siteId.
Get a specific site.
Update site name or notes.
Delete a site. This is permanent.
List buildings. ADMIN sees all buildings in the organisation. Photographer accounts only see buildings where they currently have an accepted assignment. Optional filter: ?siteId=
Create a building. ADMIN only. Saves buildingId. If the selected site already has assignment history, the response may include assignmentAdvisory for portal prompting.

item.name: Create Building In Existing Site (advisory example)

exec: pm.test('Building created for advisory check', () => pm.response.to.have.status(201));

request.description: Use this after a site already has assignment context. The response may include assignmentAdvisory so the portal can decide whether to keep the same photographer or choose another one.
Get a specific building.
Update building details.
List all floors for a building.

Request a signed upload URL for the building certificate PDF. Requires the active survey fieldwork to be completed by the photographer; in the current flow that happens via `POST /v1/buildings/:buildingId/surveys/:surveyId/complete-fieldwork`. Building certificate upload is still blocked until the active survey has at least one door and every door in that survey is already `CERTIFIED`.

Register the building certificate and set the building to CERTIFIED. Requires the active survey fieldwork to be completed by the photographer first. Building certificate registration is still blocked until the active survey has at least one door and every door in that survey is already `CERTIFIED`.
Get a pre-signed URL to download the building certificate PDF.

Delete the building certificate. Removes the PDF from GCS and deletes the certificate record so a new one can be uploaded. Admin must do this before reopening completed fieldwork or downgrading any certified door in the active survey. Returns 204 No Content on success. Only callable by ADMIN.

item.name: 🧭 Assignment Workflow
item.description: Admin-side assignment creation, grouping, reassignment, and history.

exec: pm.test('Site created for assignment flow', () => pm.response.to.have.status(201));

request.description: Creates a clean site for testing assignment and grouped invitation flows.

item.name: Create Primary Building For Assignment Flow

request.description: Creates the main building used for single assignment, reassignment, and complete/reopen examples.

item.name: Create Secondary Building For Grouped Flow

request.description: Creates a second building under the same site so grouped and bulk assignment flows can be tested.
Assign one building to one photographer without surveyId (legacy/transitional active-work flow). For planned survey-cycle assignment use the dedicated surveyId request below.

item.name: Assign Single Building To Planned Survey (with surveyId)

request.description: Assign to a specific survey version by passing surveyId. Use this for PLANNED next-cycle invitations before activation. Emits BUILDING_ASSIGNMENT_INVITED payload with buildingId/surveyId/surveyVersion.
Assign multiple selected buildings in one request. If the buildings belong to one site, the response may include grouped invitation metadata.
Assign all current buildings under the selected site. Future buildings added later are not auto-assigned.
Reassign the current building. Old access is removed immediately and the new photographer gets a fresh pending assignment.

exec: pm.test('History row includes survey metadata fields', () => {

request.description: Portal history view. Change filters to inspect assignment, rejection, reassignment, completion, and reopen events. History rows now include survey metadata fields where available (surveyId, surveyVersion, surveyStatus, surveyExecutionStatus).

item.name: 🏠 Floors & Doors
item.description:
Admin reads/edits for floor and door structure, images, and door certificates.

Admin review flow: submitted doors can be certified or reopened for changes; certified doors must drop back to SUBMITTED before reopening to DRAFT.

request.description: Get a specific floor.
Update floor label or notes.
Delete a floor.
List all doors on a floor.

Fetch the current door detail, including status, photo count, certificate presence, and timestamps. Use the `status` field to decide whether photo edits are allowed: DRAFT = editable, SUBMITTED/CERTIFIED = locked.
Update door code or notes. ADMIN only.

Returns all images for the door, each with:
- `downloadUrl` + `downloadUrlExpiresAt` — signed URL for the original (15 min)
- `downloadUrlThumb` + `downloadUrlThumbExpiresAt` — signed URL for the 400×400 JPEG thumbnail (7 days)

**Client caching strategy:**
Cache URLs keyed by `imageId`. Check `downloadUrlExpiresAt` before use — re-fetch this list (or call the single-image signed-download endpoint) if expiring within 60 seconds.

exec: // Use last image for delete so imageId stays valid for signed-download

request.description:
Fetches fresh signed download URLs for a single image.

Use this when the cached `downloadUrl` from `List Door Images` is about to expire (check `downloadUrlExpiresAt` — refresh 60 s before expiry).

Returns:
- `downloadUrl` + `downloadUrlExpiresAt` (15 min expiry)
- `downloadUrlThumb` + `downloadUrlThumbExpiresAt` (7 day expiry)
- `role` and `label`

Permanently deletes 1–20 door images in a single call.

**What happens:**
1. Validates all imageIds belong to this door and org
2. Deletes original + thumbnail files from GCS (best-effort)
3. Removes DB records in a single transaction
4. Writes an `DOOR_IMAGE_DELETED` audit log entry for each image (with full metadata snapshot)

**Access:** Admin can delete any image. Photographer can only delete images they uploaded.

**Workflow rule:** Allowed only while the door is in DRAFT. Submitted or certified doors must be reopened before photos can be deleted.

**To get imageIds:** call `List Door Images` first and copy the `id` fields.

Request a signed upload URL for a door certificate PDF. The door must already be SUBMITTED or CERTIFIED; DRAFT doors cannot be certified. Use this during admin review after the photographer has submitted the door.
Register the uploaded certificate and mark the door as CERTIFIED. Allowed for SUBMITTED doors, and can also replace the certificate on an already CERTIFIED door while the survey is still active.
Get a signed URL to download the door certificate PDF.

item.name: Delete Door Certificate (Admin only) — back to SUBMITTED

request.description: Delete the door certificate. Removes the PDF from GCS, deletes the certificate record, and resets the door status from CERTIFIED back to SUBMITTED. If photo changes are needed after that, call `Reopen Door for Changes (Admin only)` to move the door from SUBMITTED to DRAFT. If survey fieldwork had already been completed, that reopen call also reopens the active survey. If a building certificate already exists for the active survey, delete that building certificate first. Returns 204 No Content on success. Only callable by ADMIN.

Moves a submitted door back to DRAFT so the photographer can edit photos again. Use this when review finds issues after submission. If the door is CERTIFIED, first delete the certificate to return it to SUBMITTED, then call this request to reach DRAFT. If survey fieldwork had already been completed, this call also reopens the active survey back to `IN_PROGRESS`. If a building certificate already exists for the active survey, delete that building certificate first.

item.name: 📋 Survey Lifecycle
item.description: Admin survey history, activation, scheduling, completion, and reopen controls.

request.description: List all survey versions for a building in version order (oldest first). Use this for the Survey History tab. Saves surveyId from the first result.

exec: pm.test('No active survey (404 expected on completed buildings)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(404));

request.description: Get the current ACTIVE survey for a building. Returns 404 if no active survey exists (all surveys are completed and start-next hasn't been called yet). Saves currentSurveyId.
Get full read-only detail of any survey (active or completed). Includes all floors and doors with statuses and image/cert counts. Use surveyId saved from List Survey History.

item.name: Get Historical Building Cert Download URL

request.description: Get a signed download URL for the building certificate of a specific (historical) survey. Use in the history detail view.

item.name: Confirm Survey Complete + Schedule Planned Next (Admin only)

exec: console.log('Survey v' + json.data.version + ' is now COMPLETED');
console.log('Error (expected if pre-conditions not met):', json.message);

request.description: Complete the current ACTIVE survey and schedule the next cycle in the same call. Requires: active survey executionStatus=INSPECTOR_COMPLETED, building certificate registered for active survey, and all doors in that survey CERTIFIED. When scheduling is provided, backend creates plannedNextSurvey (status=PLANNED). Emits SURVEY_COMPLETED payload with buildingId/surveyId/surveyVersion.

exec: console.log('Error (expected if pre-conditions not met):', json.message);

request.description: Complete the current ACTIVE survey without scheduling the next cycle. No planned next survey row is created.

exec: console.log('Survey v' + json.data.version + ' planned — cloned ' + json.data.floorsCloned + ' floors, ' + json.data.doorsCloned + ' doors');

request.description: Create the next survey cycle as PLANNED (admin only). Clones floors/doors only from last completed survey, does not activate, does not create assignment, and does not reset building status yet. Saves plannedSurveyId.

exec: console.log('Activation blocked (expected if assignment not yet accepted):', json.message);

request.description: Activate a planned survey version. Requires accepted assignment linked to plannedSurveyId. On success, survey becomes ACTIVE and building status is reset for the new cycle. Emits SURVEY_ACTIVATED payload with buildingId/surveyId/surveyVersion.
Admin reopens an active survey whose fieldwork was marked complete, making it editable again. Emits SURVEY_FIELDWORK_REOPENED payload with buildingId/surveyId/surveyVersion.

Update scheduling metadata on the current active survey (admin only). All fields are optional. Pass null to clear a field. If nextAssignedInspectorId is provided, a push notification is sent to that photographer.

exec: pm.test('Export job created — exportJobId saved', () => pm.response.to.have.status(201));

request.description: Start a background job to build a ZIP of site, building, floor, or door survey data. Saves exportJobId.
Check status: QUEUED → RUNNING → DONE (or FAILED). Poll every few seconds.
Get a signed URL to download the ZIP. Only works when status is DONE.

request.description: Returns the organisation the current user belongs to.
Get a specific user by their ID.

Register a Firebase push notification token for this device. Call after login on mobile apps.

item.name: 🧭 Assignment Inbox
item.description: Pending and accepted assignment flows for the photographer app.

exec: pm.test('Survey metadata fields present for assignment item', () => {

request.description: Mobile inbox view. Response now separates pending, acceptedActive, and acceptedPlanned buckets so mobile can keep planned accepted surveys in a waiting-for-activation state instead of treating them as active work.
Accept one pending assignment. This unlocks workflow access for that building.
Reject one pending assignment. Once rejected, the building should stay inaccessible in workflow endpoints.
Accept all pending buildings that belong to the same grouped site invitation.
Reject all pending buildings in the same grouped invitation.
Read-only completed survey history for the current photographer. Use this after admin confirms a survey complete, because completed work intentionally disappears from the active assignment inbox.

Lightweight read-only detail for one completed survey previously worked on by the current photographer. Includes building/site metadata plus frozen floor and door summary for that survey version. Heavy assets should be loaded from the dedicated completed-survey asset endpoints.
Read-only building certificate download URL for one completed survey previously worked on by the current photographer.
Read-only door image snapshot for one completed survey door. Returns signed read URLs and should be called only when the user opens that specific completed door.
Read-only door certificate download URL for one completed survey door previously worked on by the current photographer.
Photographer history timeline. Change state filter to review accepted, rejected, removed, completed, and reopened events. Responses include survey context fields when available.

item.name: 🏗️ Accessible Sites & Buildings
item.description: Read endpoints for only the sites and buildings the photographer can access.

request.description: List sites. ADMIN sees all sites in the organisation. Photographer accounts only see sites that currently contain accepted building assignments.
Get a specific site.
List buildings. ADMIN sees all buildings in the organisation. Photographer accounts only see buildings where they currently have an accepted assignment. Optional filter: ?siteId=
Get a specific building.
List all floors for a building.
Get a pre-signed URL to download the building certificate PDF.
List all survey versions for a building in version order (oldest first). Use this for the Survey History tab. Saves surveyId from the first result.

exec: pm.test('No active survey (404 expected on completed buildings)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(404));

request.description: Get the current ACTIVE survey for a building. Returns 404 if no active survey exists (all surveys are completed and start-next hasn't been called yet). Saves currentSurveyId.
Get full read-only detail of any survey (active or completed). Includes all floors and doors with statuses and image/cert counts. Use surveyId saved from List Survey History.

item.name: Get Historical Building Cert Download URL

request.description: Get a signed download URL for the building certificate of a specific (historical) survey. Use in the history detail view.

item.name: 📸 Fieldwork & Uploads
item.description:
Floor creation, door work, image uploads, approvals, and active survey completion.

Photo upload/register/delete is DRAFT-only. After `Submit Door`, the door is locked until an admin reopens it.

Use `Bulk Submit Selected Doors (Photographer)` for partial progress and `Complete Survey Fieldwork (Photographer)` as the single final building-ready signal for the active survey.

exec: pm.test('Accepted inspector can create floor — 201', () => pm.response.to.have.status(201));

request.description: Use this after the photographer has accepted the assignment for buildingId. Saves floorId and inspectorFloorId.
Get a specific floor.
List all doors on a floor.

exec: pm.test('Accepted inspector can create door — 201', () => pm.response.to.have.status(201));

request.description: Use this after the photographer has accepted the assignment and has a valid floorId in that building. Saves doorId and inspectorDoorId.

Fetch the current door detail, including status, photo count, certificate presence, and timestamps. Use the `status` field to decide whether photo edits are allowed: DRAFT = editable, SUBMITTED/CERTIFIED = locked.

item.name: Request Image Upload URL (Photographer, DRAFT only)

request.description: Request a signed upload URL for a door image. Use this only after the photographer has accepted the building assignment, has access to the current doorId, and the door status is DRAFT. Once the door is submitted or certified, photo edits are locked until an admin reopens the door.

item.name: Register Door Image (after GCS upload, DRAFT only)

request.description: Register the uploaded image after using the signed upload URL. This request is part of the accepted-photographer workflow and is allowed only while the door is in DRAFT. After submit, admins must reopen the door before any photo changes can be registered.

exec: // If GCS signed-upload failed (no imageId set), use placeholder values
// so Register Image can still run and allow door submission

item.name: Upload Image to GCS (PUT signed URL)

exec: // This request must be run manually — paste the signedUrl from Step 1 into the {{signedUploadUrl}} env variable.
// Skipped automatically during Newman runs when no real URL is set.
// Remove Authorization header — GCS signed URLs must NOT receive a Bearer token.

header.description: Must exactly match the contentType you passed to signed-upload (e.g. image/jpeg, image/png, image/heic)

request.description:
STEP 2 of 3 — Upload the image file directly to Google Cloud Storage using the signed URL.

How to use this request:
1. Copy the 'signedUrl' value from the 'Request Image Upload URL' response
2. Paste it into the request URL above (replace {{signedUploadUrl}} or set it as an environment variable)
3. Set Content-Type header to match what you requested (e.g. image/jpeg)
4. In Body tab → select 'binary' → click 'Select File' → pick your image file
5. Send — GCS returns 200 OK with an empty body if successful

⚠️  IMPORTANT: Do NOT add an Authorization header here. The signed URL itself is the authentication. Adding a Bearer token will cause a 403 error.

⚠️  The signed URL expires in 15 minutes. If you get a 403, go back to Step 1 and get a new URL.

➜ NEXT: Call 'Register Door Image (after GCS upload)' with the imageId and objectPath from Step 1 to save the record in the database.

item.name: Batch Request Image Upload URLs (DRAFT only)

request.description: Requests signed upload URLs for multiple photos in one step. Allowed only while the door is in DRAFT. Submitted or certified doors must be reopened before more photos can be added.

exec: pm.test('Batch signed URLs returned — ' + json.data.length + ' items', () => {

item.name: Batch Register Image URLs (after GCS uploads, DRAFT only)

request.description: Registers multiple uploaded door images after GCS upload. Allowed only while the door is in DRAFT. Submitted or certified doors must be reopened before more photos can be registered.

Returns all images for the door, each with:
- `downloadUrl` + `downloadUrlExpiresAt` — signed URL for the original (15 min)
- `downloadUrlThumb` + `downloadUrlThumbExpiresAt` — signed URL for the 400×400 JPEG thumbnail (7 days)

**Client caching strategy:**
Cache URLs keyed by `imageId`. Check `downloadUrlExpiresAt` before use — re-fetch this list (or call the single-image signed-download endpoint) if expiring within 60 seconds.

- `role` and `label`

Permanently deletes 1–20 door images in a single call.

**What happens:**
1. Validates all imageIds belong to this door and org
2. Deletes original + thumbnail files from GCS (best-effort)
3. Removes DB records in a single transaction
4. Writes an `DOOR_IMAGE_DELETED` audit log entry for each image (with full metadata snapshot)

**Access:** Admin can delete any image. Photographer can only delete images they uploaded.

**Workflow rule:** Allowed only while the door is in DRAFT. Submitted or certified doors must be reopened before photos can be deleted.

**To get imageIds:** call `List Door Images` first and copy the `id` fields.

item.name: Submit Door (Photographer) — requires ≥1 image, locks photos

request.description: Accepted photographer submits the current door after at least one image has been registered. This moves the door to SUBMITTED and locks all photo edits until an admin reopens the door.
Get a signed URL to download the door certificate PDF.

Bulk-submit selected ready doors without completing the survey. Only selected DRAFT doors with at least one image are submitted. Already submitted/certified doors and draft doors with no images are returned in blockedDoors with reasons. This endpoint does not mark fieldwork complete.

Preview active survey fieldwork readiness before final completion. Returns counts plus door summaries for already submitted/certified doors, draft doors that can be bulk-submitted because they already have images, and draft doors that still block completion because they have no images.

Photographer marks fieldwork complete for the active survey version. This is the single final “building is ready for admin review” action in the current workflow. It sets survey executionStatus to `INSPECTOR_COMPLETED` and marks the building ready for certificate upload. By default, this requires at least one door in the active survey and no doors left in `DRAFT`. If some draft doors already have images, send `{ "autoSubmitValidDoors": true }` to bulk-submit those valid draft doors during the same call. The request still fails if any draft door has no images.

Delete the building certificate. Removes the PDF from GCS and deletes the certificate record so a new one can be uploaded. Admin must do this before reopening completed fieldwork or downgrading any certified door in the active survey. Returns 204 No Content on success. Only callable by ADMIN.

item.name: Request Image Upload URL (Photographer, DRAFT only)

request.description: Request a signed upload URL for a door image. Use this only after the photographer has accepted the building assignment, has access to the current doorId, and the door status is DRAFT. Once the door is submitted or certified, photo edits are locked until an admin reopens the door.

item.name: Register Door Image (after GCS upload, DRAFT only)

request.description: Register the uploaded image after using the signed upload URL. This request is part of the accepted-photographer workflow and is allowed only while the door is in DRAFT. After submit, admins must reopen the door before any photo changes can be registered.

item.name: Upload Image to GCS (PUT signed URL)

exec: // This request must be run manually — paste the signedUrl from Step 1 into the {{signedUploadUrl}} env variable.
// Skipped automatically during Newman runs when no real URL is set.
// Remove Authorization header — GCS signed URLs must NOT receive a Bearer token.

header.description: Must exactly match the contentType you passed to signed-upload (e.g. image/jpeg, image/png, image/heic)

request.description:
STEP 2 of 3 — Upload the image file directly to Google Cloud Storage using the signed URL.

How to use this request:
1. Copy the 'signedUrl' value from the 'Request Image Upload URL' response
2. Paste it into the request URL above (replace {{signedUploadUrl}} or set it as an environment variable)
3. Set Content-Type header to match what you requested (e.g. image/jpeg)
4. In Body tab → select 'binary' → click 'Select File' → pick your image file
5. Send — GCS returns 200 OK with an empty body if successful

⚠️  IMPORTANT: Do NOT add an Authorization header here. The signed URL itself is the authentication. Adding a Bearer token will cause a 403 error.

⚠️  The signed URL expires in 15 minutes. If you get a 403, go back to Step 1 and get a new URL.

➜ NEXT: Call 'Register Door Image (after GCS upload)' with the imageId and objectPath from Step 1 to save the record in the database.

item.name: Batch Request Image Upload URLs (DRAFT only)

request.description: Requests signed upload URLs for multiple photos in one step. Allowed only while the door is in DRAFT. Submitted or certified doors must be reopened before more photos can be added.

exec: pm.test('Batch signed URLs returned — ' + json.data.length + ' items', () => {

item.name: Batch Register Image URLs (after GCS uploads, DRAFT only)

request.description: Registers multiple uploaded door images after GCS upload. Allowed only while the door is in DRAFT. Submitted or certified doors must be reopened before more photos can be registered.

Returns all images for the door, each with:
- `downloadUrl` + `downloadUrlExpiresAt` — signed URL for the original (15 min)
- `downloadUrlThumb` + `downloadUrlThumbExpiresAt` — signed URL for the 400×400 JPEG thumbnail (7 days)

**Client caching strategy:**
Cache URLs keyed by `imageId`. Check `downloadUrlExpiresAt` before use — re-fetch this list (or call the single-image signed-download endpoint) if expiring within 60 seconds.

exec: // Use last image for delete so imageId stays valid for signed-download

request.description:
Fetches fresh signed download URLs for a single image.

Use this when the cached `downloadUrl` from `List Door Images` is about to expire (check `downloadUrlExpiresAt` — refresh 60 s before expiry).

Returns:
- `downloadUrl` + `downloadUrlExpiresAt` (15 min expiry)
- `downloadUrlThumb` + `downloadUrlThumbExpiresAt` (7 day expiry)
- `role` and `label`

Permanently deletes 1–20 door images in a single call.

**What happens:**
1. Validates all imageIds belong to this door and org
2. Deletes original + thumbnail files from GCS (best-effort)
3. Removes DB records in a single transaction
4. Writes an `DOOR_IMAGE_DELETED` audit log entry for each image (with full metadata snapshot)

**Access:** Admin can delete any image. Photographer can only delete images they uploaded.

**Workflow rule:** Allowed only while the door is in DRAFT. Submitted or certified doors must be reopened before photos can be deleted.

**To get imageIds:** call `List Door Images` first and copy the `id` fields.

item.name: Submit Door (Photographer) — requires ≥1 image, locks photos

request.description: Accepted photographer submits the current door after at least one image has been registered. This moves the door to SUBMITTED and locks all photo edits until an admin reopens the door.

Request a signed upload URL for a door certificate PDF. The door must already be SUBMITTED or CERTIFIED; DRAFT doors cannot be certified. Use this during admin review after the photographer has submitted the door.
Register the uploaded certificate and mark the door as CERTIFIED. Allowed for SUBMITTED doors, and can also replace the certificate on an already CERTIFIED door while the survey is still active.
Get a signed URL to download the door certificate PDF.

item.name: Delete Door Certificate (Admin only) — back to SUBMITTED

request.description: Delete the door certificate. Removes the PDF from GCS, deletes the certificate record, and resets the door status from CERTIFIED back to SUBMITTED. If photo changes are needed after that, call `Reopen Door for Changes (Admin only)` to move the door from SUBMITTED to DRAFT. If survey fieldwork had already been completed, that reopen call also reopens the active survey. If a building certificate already exists for the active survey, delete that building certificate first. Returns 204 No Content on success. Only callable by ADMIN.

Moves a submitted door back to DRAFT so the photographer can edit photos again. Use this when review finds issues after submission. If the door is CERTIFIED, first delete the certificate to return it to SUBMITTED, then call this request to reach DRAFT. If survey fieldwork had already been completed, this call also reopens the active survey back to `IN_PROGRESS`. If a building certificate already exists for the active survey, delete that building certificate first.

Requires: active survey executionStatus=INSPECTOR_COMPLETED, building certificate registered for active survey, and all doors in that survey CERTIFIED. When scheduling is provided, backend creates plannedNextSurvey (status=PLANNED). Emits SURVEY_COMPLETED payload with buildingId/surveyId/surveyVersion.

exec: console.log('Activation blocked (expected if assignment not yet accepted):', json.message);

request.description: Activate a planned survey version. Requires accepted assignment linked to plannedSurveyId. On success, survey becomes ACTIVE and building status is reset for the new cycle. Emits SURVEY_ACTIVATED payload with buildingId/surveyId/surveyVersion.

Bulk-submit selected ready doors without completing the survey. Only selected DRAFT doors with at least one image are submitted. Already submitted/certified doors and draft doors with no images are returned in blockedDoors with reasons. This endpoint does not mark fieldwork complete.

Preview active survey fieldwork readiness before final completion. Returns counts plus door summaries for already submitted/certified doors, draft doors that can be bulk-submitted because they already have images, and draft doors that still block completion because they have no images.

Photographer marks fieldwork complete for the active survey version. This is the single final “building is ready for admin review” action in the current workflow. It sets survey executionStatus to `INSPECTOR_COMPLETED` and marks the building ready for certificate upload. By default, this requires at least one door in the active survey and no doors left in `DRAFT`. If some draft doors already have images, send `{ "autoSubmitValidDoors": true }` to bulk-submit those valid draft doors during the same call. The request still fails if any draft door has no images.
Admin reopens an active survey whose fieldwork was marked complete, making it editable again. Emits SURVEY_FIELDWORK_REOPENED payload with buildingId/surveyId/surveyVersion.

Update scheduling metadata on the current active survey (admin only). All fields are optional. Pass null to clear a field. If nextAssignedInspectorId is provided, a push notification is sent to that photographer.
Check server and database health. Returns { status: 'ok' } when everything is running.

item.name: 🧪 QA / Access Control
item.description: Negative and authorization-focused checks. Keep these separate from the normal admin portal and photographer workflow folders.

item.name: 🚫 Forbidden Writes
